title: Aerial Firefighting Enhancement Act of 2025
stage_label: Senate Committee on Armed Services discharged by Unanimous Consent.
plain_summary: This law lets the military sell old aircraft and aircraft parts to groups that fight wildfires. The aircraft can drop water or special chemicals on fires to put them out. The military can do this from October 2025 until October 2035. The groups that buy these aircraft can now also use them to help fight fires in other countries, which they could not do before.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Aerial Firefighting Enhancement Act of two thousand and twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Modification and Reauthorization of Authority for Sale by Department of Defense of Aircraft and Parts for Wildfire Suppression Purposes
sections.text: This section changes an existing law from nineteen ninety six called the Wildfire Suppression Aircraft Transfer Act. The changes allow the Department of Defense (the military) to sell aircraft and aircraft parts to help fight wildfires. The law now says these aircraft can drop both fire retardant (chemicals that slow down fires) or water on fires. Any aircraft sold through this program can only be used to provide aircraft services for fighting wildfires. The authority to sell these aircraft and parts will last from the day this new law is signed until October first, two thousand and thirty five.
